Hooks describe event-driven automation boundaries. GitClaw treats hook specs as reviewed metadata only: no hook handler is executed, no workflow is dispatched, and no hook, issue, comment, or provider payload body is printed by this report.

### Hook Policy
- `.gitclaw/HOOKS.md` present=`true` bytes=`882` lines=`23` sha256_12=`ec07027d7e51`
- `.gitclaw/HOOKS.md` loaded=`true` source=`contextDocumentPaths`

### Hook Specs
- name=`repo-hygiene-audit` path=`.gitclaw/hooks/repo-hygiene.md` bytes=`319` lines=`14` frontmatter=`true` events=`2` mode=`audit-only` delivery=`issue-comment` requires_approval=`true` sha256_12=`10ca73c60997`

### Runtime Boundary
- hook handlers are not executed by `gitclaw handle`
- executable-looking hook files are reported as ignored metadata, not run
- future executable hooks require explicit workflow permissions, approval gates, and body-free audit cards
- GitHub-native hook effects should use reviewed workflows or `workflow_dispatch`, never untrusted issue text as code

### Executable Handler Files
- none

### Verification Findings
- none
